summary:
You must complete numCourses courses labeled 0 to numCourses - 1. The list prerequisites contains pairs [a, b] meaning course b has to be taken before course a.

Decide whether it is possible to finish every course. This is possible exactly when the prerequisite relationships contain no cycle.

examples.explanation: Take course 0 first, then course 1. There is no conflicting dependency.
Course 1 needs course 0 and course 0 needs course 1, a cycle that can never be satisfied.

constraints: Each prerequisites[i] = [a, b] with 0 <= a, b < numCourses
Return true only if all courses can be completed (the dependency graph is acyclic)
